question: How do you ask 'Where is the bathroom?' in Latvian?
correctAnswer: {{lv-LV|Kur ir tualete?||KUR IR TUA-le-te?}}
explanation: 'Kur ir' = 'where is'. Replace 'tualete' with any destination: stacija, viesnīca, etc.

question: How do you ask 'How much does this cost?' in Latvian?
correctAnswer: {{lv-LV|Cik tas maksā?||CIK TAS MAK-sa?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{lv-LV|Vai tas ir labs?||VAI TAS IR LABS?}}
explanation: 'Cik tas maksā?' — 'How much does this cost?' — essential for markets and shops.

question: How do you order 'a coffee, please' in Latvian?
correctAnswer: {{lv-LV|Vienu kafiju, lūdzu.||VIE-nu KA-fi-ju, LUD-zu.}}
explanation: 'Vienu [item], lūdzu' — 'One [item], please' — the standard café order pattern.

question: How do you ask for the bill in Latvian?
correctAnswer: {{lv-LV|Rēķinu, lūdzu.||RE-ki-nu, LUD-zu.}}
explanation: 'Rēķinu, lūdzu' — 'The bill, please' — straightforward and polite.

question: How do you say 'I don't understand' in Latvian?
correctAnswer: {{lv-LV|Es nesaprotu.||ES NE-sa-pro-tu.}}
explanation: 'Es nesaprotu' — 'I don't understand' — the essential phrase when you're lost.

question: How do you say 'Do you speak English?' in Latvian?
correctAnswer: {{lv-LV|Vai jūs runājat angliski?||VAI JUS RU-na-jat AN-glis-ki?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{lv-LV|Vai jūs saprotat angliski?||VAI JUS SA-pro-tat AN-glis-ki?}}
{{lv-LV|Vai jūs zināt angliski?||VAI JUS ZI-nat AN-glis-ki?}}
{{lv-LV|Vai jūs mācāties angliski?||VAI JUS MA-ca-ties AN-glis-ki?}}
explanation: 'Vai jūs runājat angliski?' — the polite plural/formal form. Informal: 'Vai tu runā angliski?'

question: How do you ask 'Where is the bus stop?' in Latvian?
correctAnswer: {{lv-LV|Kur ir autobusa pietura?||KUR IR AU-to-bu-sa PIE-tu-ra?}}
explanation: 'Autobusa pietura' = bus stop. 'Tramvaja pietura' = tram stop, 'trolejbusa pietura' = trolleybus stop.

question: In beginner Latvian, how do you say 'Good evening.'?
correctAnswer: {{lv-LV|Labvakar.||LAB-va-kar.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{lv-LV|Man ir labi, paldies.||MAN IR LA-bi, PAL-dies.}}
explanation: This is a common greeting or farewell used as a fixed chunk in everyday conversation. Memorizing it whole makes it easier to sound natural.

question: In beginner Latvian, how do you say 'I have a reservation.'?
correctAnswer: {{lv-LV|Man ir rezervācija.||MAN IR RE-zer-va-ci-ja.}}
incorrectAnswers: {{lv-LV|Vai jums ir vienvietīga istaba?||VAI JUMS IR VIEN-vie-ti-ga IS-ta-ba?}}
{{lv-LV|Vai jums ir divvietīga istaba?||VAI JUMS IR DIV-vie-ti-ga IS-ta-ba?}}
explanation: This is standard accommodation language for front-desk conversations. It is polite and easy to reuse.

question: Which Latvian phrase means 'Do you have a single room?'?
correctAnswer: {{lv-LV|Vai jums ir vienvietīga istaba?||VAI JUMS IR VIEN-vie-ti-ga IS-ta-ba?}}
incorrectAnswers: {{lv-LV|Vai jums ir divvietīga istaba?||VAI JUMS IR DIV-vie-ti-ga IS-ta-ba?}}
{{lv-LV|Ar istabu ir problēma.||AR IS-ta-bu IR PRO-ble-ma.}}
explanation: This is standard accommodation language for front-desk conversations. It is polite and easy to reuse.
